differential_diagnoses: Cushing's syndrome due to ectopic ACTH production (e.g., occult malignancy) leading to profound immunosuppression
Adrenocortical carcinoma with metastatic central nervous system involvement
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF fungal culture, cryptococcal antigen, Tuberculous PCR, AFB smear/culture, and cytology
Measure serum ACTH, DHEAS, and midnight salivary cortisol to confirm and characterize the cause of hypercortisolism
Obtain thoracic and abdominal CT imaging (re-evaluation for occult ectopic ACTH source)
Initiate empiric antifungal therapy (e.g., liposomal amphotericin B and flucytosine) given high clinical suspicion for fungal meningitis
Consult neurology for evaluation of VNS compatibility with high-field MRI or consideration of temporary disabling of the device for imaging
Assess serum beta-D-glucan and Histoplasma antigen levels
